title: Daily Briefing — Thursday, June 4
tldr: Play: turn your own documents into a grounded, cited Q&A source with NotebookLM. Stop scrolling PDFs and reports — upload them and ask questions answered only from your sources, with citations you can click.
The day's headlines (an AI Act draft, an executive order, IPO filings) were policy and corporate news — cut. The durable, run-it-today move is the research assistant below.

sections.h: Run it today: a private research assistant grounded in your docs

blocks.sub: Ask your documents questions instead of re-reading them
blocks.p: Payoff: fast, trustworthy answers pulled only from material you uploaded — no open-web hallucination — with inline citations back to the exact passage. Ideal for contracts, research bundles, meeting notes, or a competitor's whitepapers.
blocks.doIt: Open NotebookLM (free, Google account). Create a notebook and upload your sources (PDFs, Google Docs, pasted text, a website or YouTube URL). Ask in plain language — What are the renewal terms? or Summarize the risks across all three docs — and every claim links to its source. Use the Audio Overview to turn the set into a podcast-style briefing.
blocks.note: Because it answers only from your sources, it's far less likely to make things up than a general chatbot.
